name: Molino de Viñuelas — circular from Viñuelas
name_original: Molino de Viñuelas — circular desde Viñuelas
region_intro: Jogging around Viñuelas offers diverse landscapes, from the gentle relief of the Corredor de Colmenar to the Axarquía mountains. The region features olive groves, cereal fields, and the prominent Lake Viñuela, the largest reservoir in Málaga province. Downstream from the dam, the terrain becomes more rugged, with slate hills adorned with scrub, holm oaks, olive trees, and vineyards, providing varied running routes. Best jogging routes around Viñuelas The most popular jogging route is Molino de Viñuelas — circular from Viñuelas, an easy 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes about 30 minutes to complete. This route is mostly paved and suitable for various fitness levels. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from El Cubillo de Uceda, a moderate 4.9 miles (7.9 km) path. This route is mostly paved and requires good fitness. Local runners also love the Running loop from Parque de las Castillas, a 3.5 miles (5.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 35 minutes. Jogging around Viñuelas is defined by olive groves, the scenic Lake Viñuela, and rolling hills, with options for different ability levels. The routes in Viñuelas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 20 runners have used komoot to explore Viñuelas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Viñuelas?
A: Viñuelas offers a selection of running routes, with a total of 5 trails documented in this guide. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Viñuelas?
A: The running trails in Viñuelas feature diverse landscapes. You'll encounter gentle relief with olive groves and cereal fields, as well as more rugged terrain downstream from the dam, characterized by slate hills, scrub, holm oaks, and vineyards. Many routes are mostly paved, offering a smooth running experience.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Viñuelas?
A: The running routes in Viñuelas are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 20 runners have already explored Viñuelas's varied terrain using komoot, appreciating the scenic beauty and diverse options.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Viñuelas?
A: Yes, Viñuelas has easy running options perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032426567/molino-de-vinuelas-circular-from-vinuelas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de Viñuelas — circular from Viñuelas&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 2.7-mile (4.4 km) trail that is mostly paved and takes about 30 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there more challenging running routes in Viñuelas for experienced runners?
A: For runners seeking a moderate challenge, Viñuelas offers several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10417104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from El Cubillo de Uceda&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.9-mile (7.9 km) path, mostly paved, requiring good fitness. Another moderate choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38242221" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Parque de las Castillas&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.5 miles (5.6 km) and leads through varied terrain.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Viñuelas?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Viñuelas are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032426567/molino-de-vinuelas-circular-from-vinuelas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de Viñuelas — circular from Viñuelas&lt;/a&gt; is an easy loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10417104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from El Cubillo de Uceda&lt;/a&gt; also provides a circular experience.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see while running in Viñuelas?
A: Viñuelas is rich in natural beauty. You can enjoy views of the stunning &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vi%C3%B1uela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Viñuela&lt;/a&gt;, the largest reservoir in Málaga province, with the majestic Sierra Tejeda and La Maroma in the distance. The landscape is also characterized by picturesque olive groves, cereal fields, and areas with scrub, holm oaks, and vineyards.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the running trails?
A: Yes, you can encounter interesting landmarks. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2833315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viñuelas Mill&lt;/a&gt; is a picturesque feature in the area. While running, you might also pass through areas with ancient Arabic irrigation channels (acequias) and old mills, reflecting the region's rich history.

Q: When is the best time of year to go running in Viñuelas?
A: The best time to go running in Viñuelas is typically during the spring and autumn months when the weather is milder. Summers can be very hot, especially in the middle of the day, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler temperatures. Spring also brings vibrant wildflowers to the landscape.

Q: Is parking available near the running routes in Viñuelas?
A: While specific parking details for each route are not always provided, Viñuelas is a rural area, and parking is generally available in or near the villages that serve as starting points for many trails. It's advisable to check local signage or maps for designated parking areas.

Q: Are there family-friendly running paths in Viñuelas?
A: Yes, Viñuelas offers family-friendly running paths, particularly the easier, mostly paved routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032426567/molino-de-vinuelas-circular-from-vinuelas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de Viñuelas — circular from Viñuelas&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice for families due to its easy difficulty and relatively short distance.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Viñuelas?
A: Many outdoor trails in Spain are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land. Please be mindful of local regulations and ensure your dog is well-behaved and doesn't disturb other users or animals.

Q: Are there running routes that offer views of Lake Viñuela?
A: Absolutely. The stunning &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vi%C3%B1uela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Viñuela&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent feature of the region, and several routes, such as the 'La Viñuela Reservoir loop from Cortijuelos' (mentioned in regional research), offer picturesque views of the turquoise reservoir and the surrounding mountains.
tips: One of the monuments of the town of Viñuelas and one of the best preserved of its kind, the old mill stands alone, offering a very picturesque landscape next to the surrounding mountains.
